Observe.Resolve.Automate.
FunctionsArchitectureUse CasesLawsToolsTrainingAbout

DRF Observability

GTP Proxy — Complete Observability Story

Three instruments. Four proofs. One guarantee.

The DRF is not just a network function — it is a revenue-critical transaction processor. Every GTP message it handles represents billable roaming revenue. Observability must prove it never drops, miscounts, or misroutes money.

System Under Test

The Data Roaming Function

A GTP-C proxy/hub sitting between Visited (VPMN) and Home (HPMN) networks. Every roaming data session passes through it. It must alias TEIDs, route across clusters, and generate a CDR for every billable event.

VPMN

Visited Network

S-GW / MME sends GTPv1 or GTPv2 to DRF

GTPv1-CGTPv2-CGTP-U

DRF (System Under Test)

DRF-C

Core GTP-C proxy logic

DRF-TIDE

TEID allocator

DRF-Router

Routing library

Valkey

Session state cache

CDRF

CDR storage

UPF

GTP-U user plane

HPMN

Home Network

P-GW / SMF receives proxied GTP from DRF

GTPv1-CGTPv2-CPFCP

Key invariant: TEID on VPMN side ≠ TEID on HPMN side — the DRF aliases every tunnel. If this aliasing breaks, sessions are misrouted and CDRs are lost.

Instrument Plane

Three Instruments, Three Angles on DRF

Independence is the architecture. Each instrument sees a different slice of the DRF's behaviour. Agreement confirms correctness. Disagreement IS the diagnostic.

Evolver

The Contract Enforcer

Executes 243 YAML scenarios against DRF. Injects synthetic GTPv1/v2 messages, validates TEID aliasing, asserts CDR structure.

Schedule

Pre-deployment CI/CD gate · Nightly Tier 1 · Weekly full suite

GTPv1

77 scenarios

GTPv2

166 scenarios

Delivers

Proof of TransformationProof of Persistence

nScan

The Production Witness

Passively taps live traffic at DRF ingress/egress. Verifies real TEID aliasing, correlates CDRs with captured sessions.

Schedule

24 × 7 capture · 1% sampled analysis · anomaly detection

GTPv1

GTP-C tap

GTPv2

GTP-U tap

Delivers

Proof of TransitProof of Persistence

ANTS

The Field Truth

RTU probes dial actual roaming sessions through the DRF from field locations. Validates end-to-end subscriber experience.

Schedule

Hourly probes · 3+ locations · 5-min failure alerting

GTPv1

GTPv1 roaming

GTPv2

GTPv2 roaming

Delivers

Proof of TransitProof of Compliance

Evidence Model

The Four Proofs

Don't just monitor — prove. Every observability output must deliver a specific burden of proof to the customer. Data without evidence is noise.

Proof of Transit

Message entered DRF at VPMN side and exited at HPMN side without being dropped.

nScanANTS

How

nScan captures GTP headers at both interfaces. ANTS verifies end-to-end session success from field.

Customer Evidence

"We can prove the message was forwarded."

Proof of Transformation

TEID aliasing worked — VPMN TEID ≠ HPMN TEID, but session state is consistent throughout.

Evolver

How

Evolver YAML assertions validate CDR contains correct aliased TEID pairs. nScan confirms at runtime.

Customer Evidence

"We can prove DRF did not confuse or leak sessions."

Proof of Persistence

A CDR was generated, written to CDRF, and contains accurate session data for every billable event.

EvolvernScanANTS

How

Evolver asserts CDR content in YAML tests. ANTS queries CDRF write completion. nScan correlates to timestamps.

Customer Evidence

"We can prove billing records are complete and accurate."

Proof of Compliance

DRF follows GSMA IR.88 guidelines — correct rejection codes, proper timer behaviour, valid CDR fields.

EvolverANTS

How

Automated IR.88 scorecard derived from Tier 1-3 results. Quarterly audit reports with traceability.

Customer Evidence

"We can prove compliance to regulators and roaming partners."

Industry Standards Alignment

Four Proofs × ARIAM QoS Framework

Revenue assurance and network performance are two views of the same system. The Four Proofs provide evidence; ARIAM metrics provide performance context. Together they satisfy both CFO audits and CTO engineering reviews.

How Revenue Proofs Map to QoS Metrics

Transit
→
Accessibility
GTP messages reach destination; network path available
Transformation
→
Integrity
TEID aliasing correct; protocol processing accurate
Persistence
→
Retainability + Maintainability
CDRs retained for billing; audit trail complete
Compliance
→
Integrity + Security
GSMA IR.88 followed; rejection handling per spec

Why ARIAM Alone Is Insufficient

Scenario: DRF shows 99.99% availability (ARIAM green). All sessions complete.

Problem: 2% of CDRs are missing due to race condition in CDRF write.

ARIAM result: System healthy. No alerts.

Four Proofs result: Persistence proof fails. CDR Completeness = 0.98. Revenue at Risk calculated.

The takeaway:

ARIAM tells you the network works. Four Proofs tell you the money is captured. Both are necessary; neither is sufficient alone.

ETSI NFV TST009GSMA IR.88TM Forum RAITU-T E.8003GPP TS 32.240

Test Strategy

243 Tests — Prioritised by Revenue Risk

Don't test everything equally. 80% of business value lives in 20% of test cases. The pyramid defines what to run when — and what a failure in each tier means.

Tier 148

Revenue-Critical

Frequency: Daily

On failure: Immediate revenue loss

Test categories

  • Create PDP/Session
  • Delete PDP/Session
  • Data Plane
  • Handover

Instruments

EvolvernScanANTS

Business Question

"Will today's deployment break roaming revenue?"

Tier 2127

Operational

Frequency: Weekly

On failure: Revenue leakage

Test categories

  • Modify Bearer
  • Update Bearer
  • Inter-Cluster
  • Resume/Suspend

Instruments

EvolvernScan

Business Question

"Are we leaking revenue through inefficiency?"

Tier 368

Edge & Compliance

Frequency: On Change

On failure: Compliance risk

Test categories

  • Rejection Scenarios
  • Malformed Packets
  • Timeout Recovery
  • Trace Session

Instruments

Evolver

Business Question

"Are we secure and compliant for audit?"

Total: 243 YAML scenarios (77 GTPv1 + 166 GTPv2) across Create/Update/Delete/Handover/Resume/Trace/Data-Plane categories. 68 tests currently NOT DEFINED represent known coverage gaps.

Closed-Loop Validation

From Detection to Confirmed Resolution

A real scenario: TEID aliasing regression introduced by a DRF-TIDE code change. Watch how all three instruments work together — and how the loop closes.

1
EvolverTier 1 — TC101

Detects TEID aliasing regression

Nightly run finds GTPv2 Create Session response contains wrong aliased TEID after DRF-TIDE code change. CDR assertion fails.

2
nScanProof of Transformation

Confirms in live production traffic

Passive capture shows 0.3% of sessions have mismatched TEIDs in live traffic. Correlated to deployment timestamp.

3
ANTSProof of Transit

Quantifies subscriber impact

Field probe from Senegal reports 12 failed roaming data sessions in the past hour. CDRF missing CDRs for those sessions.

4
Correlation—

Establishes causal chain + financial impact

TEID regression → misrouted sessions → missing CDRs → unbilled revenue. Estimated €4,200 / hour at risk.

5
DecisionL1–L4

Alert or autonomous rollback

L3/L4: DRF-TIDE rollback triggered automatically. L1/L2: on-call engineer receives correlated alert with all three instrument data.

6
EvolverTier 1 — Full Suite

Re-runs regression suite post-fix

All 48 Tier 1 YAML tests pass. TEID aliasing correct. CDR assertions validated.

7
nScanProof of Transformation

Confirms clean production traffic

Live capture shows zero TEID mismatches in 15-minute post-rollback window.

8
ANTSProof of Transit

Confirms roaming restored for subscribers

All field probes green. CDRF CDR completeness back to 1.000. Zero lost revenue in 30-min follow-up window.

Why all three instruments are required:

Evolver detects the regression in synthetic tests before production. nScan proves it is happening in real traffic. ANTS proves real subscribers are affected. Without all three, you have a theory — not a fact.

Diagnostic Power

Why Disagreement Is the Signal

When instruments agree on DRF, the picture is clear. When they disagree, the pattern tells you exactly where the fault is.

EvolverANTSnScanDRF Verdict
DRF healthy — TEID aliasing, CDR and field all confirmed
Protocol regression in DRF logic — not yet visible in production load
Field routing issue — DRF passes synthetic, real subscribers dropped
CDR leakage — sessions succeed but billing records are missing
DRF down — both active instruments fail, traffic tapped but not processed
Full DRF outage — all layers impacted, zero roaming sessions completing

Unified Scorecard

Three Metrics That Matter

Don't report 200 KPIs. Report three numbers that answer the only questions that matter.

DRF Health Score

Formula

(T1% × 40) + (T2% × 35) + (T3% × 25)

Target

≥ 95

Updated hourly

Revenue at Risk

Formula

Failed Tier 1 × Avg Session Value × Daily Volume

Target

€ 0

Updated real-time

CDR Completeness

Formula

CDRs Generated / Sessions Created

Target

1.000

Updated hourly

The DRF observability principle in one sentence:

Evolver proves the contract. nScan witnesses production. ANTS confirms the field. Together they eliminate the gap between what was tested and what subscribers experience.
